# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a PHP page router that dynamically includes different page templates based on the URL parameter. Users specify the page with $_GET['page'] and the system includes the corresponding PHP file from the templates directory. Keep it simple and flexible for our multi-page website CMS.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php

// Define the base directory for templates
define('TEMPLATE_DIR', __DIR__ . '/templates/');

// Define allowed pages (whitelist for security)
$allowed_pages = [
    'home',
    'about',
    'contact',
    'blog',
    'services',
    'portfolio',
    'faq',
    'privacy',
    'terms',
    '404'
];

// Get the requested page from URL parameter, default to 'home'
$page = isset($_GET['page']) ? $_GET['page'] : 'home';

// Sanitize the page parameter
// Remove any directory traversal attempts and special characters
$page = basename($page);
$page = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/', '', $page);

// Validate against whitelist
if (!in_array($page, $allowed_pages)) {
    $page = '404';
}

// Build the full file path
$template_file = TEMPLATE_DIR . $page . '.php';

// Verify the file exists and is within the templates directory
$real_template_path = realpath($template_file);
$real_template_dir = realpath(TEMPLATE_DIR);

// Security check: ensure the resolved path is within the templates directory
if ($real_template_path === false || strpos($real_template_path, $real_template_dir) !== 0) {
    $template_file = TEMPLATE_DIR . '404.php';
}

?>
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title><?php echo ucfirst(htmlspecialchars($page)); ?> - My CMS</title>
    <style>
        body {
            font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            background-color: #f5f5f5;
        }
        header {
            background-color: #333;
            color: #fff;
            padding: 15px 30px;
        }
        header h1 {
            margin: 0;
            display: inline-block;
        }
        nav {
            background-color: #444;
            padding: 10px 30px;
        }
        nav a {
            color: #fff;
            text-decoration: none;
            margin-right: 20px;
            padding: 5px 10px;
            border-radius: 3px;
        }
        nav a:hover, nav a.active {
            background-color: #666;
        }
        .content {
            max-width: 1200px;
            margin: 20px auto;
            padding: 20px 30px;
            background-color: #fff;
            border-radius: 5px;
            box-shadow: 0 2px 5px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);
            min-height: 400px;
        }
        footer {
            text-align: center;
            padding: 20px;
            background-color: #333;
            color: #fff;
            margin-top: 20px;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>

<header>
    <h1>My CMS Website</h1>
</header>

<nav>
    <?php
    // Generate navigation links dynamically from allowed pages (excluding 404)
    $nav_pages = array_filter($allowed_pages, function($p) {
        return $p !== '404';
    });

    foreach ($nav_pages as $nav_page):
        $active_class = ($page === $nav_page) ? ' class="active"' : '';
    ?>
        <a href="?page=<?php echo htmlspecialchars($nav_page); ?>"<?php echo $active_class; ?>>
            <?php echo ucfirst(htmlspecialchars($nav_page)); ?>
        </a>
    <?php endforeach; ?>
</nav>

<div class="content">
    <?php
    // Include the template file
    if (file_exists($template_file)) {
        include $template_file;
    } else {
        // Fallback if even the 404 template doesn't exist
        echo '<h2>Page Not Found</h2>';
        echo '<p>Sorry, the page you are looking for does not exist.</p>';
        echo '<p><a href="?page=home">Return to Home</a></p>';
    }
    ?>
</div>

<footer>
    <p>&copy; <?php echo date('Y'); ?> My CMS Website. All rights reserved.</p>
</footer>

</body>
</html>

<?php
/*
 * SETUP INSTRUCTIONS:
 * 
 * 1. Create a "templates" directory in the same folder as this file.
 * 
 * 2. Create the following template files inside the "templates" directory:
 *    - home.php
 *    - about.php
 *    - contact.php
 *    - blog.php
 *    - services.php
 *    - portfolio.php
 *    - faq.php
 *    - privacy.php
 *    - terms.php
 *    - 404.php
 * 
 * Example template content for templates/home.php:
 *    <h2>Welcome Home</h2>
 *    <p>This is the home page of our CMS.</p>
 * 
 * Example template content for templates/404.php:
 *    <h2>404 - Page Not Found</h2>
 *    <p>Sorry, the page you requested does not exist.</p>
 *    <p><a href="?page=home">Go back to Home</a></p>
 * 
 * 3. To add new pages:
 *    - Add the page name to the $allowed_pages array
 *    - Create the corresponding .php file in the templates directory
 * 
 * Usage:
 *    index.php              -> loads home template
 *    index.php?page=about   -> loads about template
 *    index.php?page=invalid -> loads 404 template
 */
?>
